\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pipeline to select for IR\sphinxhyphen{}Bright AGN in the SPT cluster surveys.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{se\_cat\_dir} \textendash{} Directory for the SExtractor photometric catalogs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{irac\_image\_dir} \textendash{} Directory for the IRAC 3.6 um and 4.5 um science and coverage images.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{region\_file\_dir} \textendash{} Directory for DS9 regions files describing areas to mask manually.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{mask\_dir} \textendash{} Directory to write good pixel masks into.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{gaia\_cat\_dir} \textendash{} Gaia stellar catalog for all relevant fields.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{spt\_catalog} \textendash{} Official SPT cluster catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{completeness\_file} \textendash{} File name of the completeness simulation results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{purity\_color\_threshold\_file} \textendash{} Filename containing the function values of the purity\sphinxhyphen{}based color selection thresholds and the associated
redshift bins.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{sed} \textendash{} Spectral energy distribution template of the source object, used for K\sphinxhyphen{}correction/absolute magnitude.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{irac\_filter} \textendash{} Filename of the IRAC filter being used for absolute magnitude calculations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{j\_band\_filter} \textendash{} Filename of the FLAMINGOS J\sphinxhyphen{}band filter being used for absolute magnitude calculations.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{completeness\_value() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{completeness\_value()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.completeness_value}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{completeness\_value}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{selection\_band}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}I2\_MAG\_APER4\textquotesingle{}}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adds completeness simulation data to the catalog.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Takes the completeness curve values for the cluster, interpolates a function between the discrete values, then
queries the specified magnitude of the selected objects in the Source Extractor catalog and adds two columns:
The completeness value of that object at its magnitude and the completeness correction value
\sphinxtitleref{(1/{[}completeness value{]})}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{selection\_band} \textendash{} Column name in Source Extractor catalog specifying the selection band to use. Must be the same band used in
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.object_selection}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object\_selection()}}}}}. Defaults to the 4.5 um, 4” aperture magnitude photometry.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{coverage\_mask() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{coverage\_mask()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.coverage_mask}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{coverage\_mask}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{ch1\_min\_cov}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{  }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{ch2\_min\_cov}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{  }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generates a binary good pixel map using the coverage maps.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates a new fits image where every pixel has values of \sphinxtitleref{1} if the coverage values in both IRAC bands are above
the given thresholds or \sphinxtitleref{0} otherwise.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ch1\_min\_cov} \textendash{} Minimum coverage value allowed in 3.6 um band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ch2\_min\_cov} \textendash{} Minimum coverage value allowed in 4.5 um band.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method writes fits images into the directory specified as \sphinxtitleref{mask\_dir} in initialization.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{file\_pairing() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{file\_pairing()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.file_pairing}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{file\_pairing}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{include}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Iterable}{Iterable}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{exclude}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Iterable}{Iterable}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Collates the different files for each cluster into a dictionary.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This will also remove any clusters we request and perform a check that all clusters have the required files.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{include} \textendash{} A list containing the names of the clusters we wish to filter for from the original sample.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{exclude} \textendash{} A list containing the names of the clusters we wish to remove from our sample.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#UserWarning}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{UserWarning}}} \textendash{} A warning is printed if a cluster is missing any necessary files. These clusters will be automatically
    removed from the sample.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The list provided by \sphinxtitleref{include} is filtered for first before the \sphinxtitleref{exclude} list is processed. This allows the
sample to be filtered for a set of clusters we want to run the selection on while still allowing us to mark
certain clusters to be removed from the sample.

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Collates all catalogs into one table then writes the catalog to disk.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{filename} \textendash{} File name of the output catalog file. If not specified, the function will return the final catalog instead
of writing to disk.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{catalog\_cols} \textendash{} List of column names in the catalog which we wish to keep in our output file. If not specified, all columns
present in the catalog are kept in the output catalog.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The final catalog of the survey AGN. If \sphinxtitleref{filename} is specified, the function will write the catalog to disk
instead of returning.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
final\_catalog

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{image\_to\_catalog\_match() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{image\_to\_catalog\_match()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.image_to_catalog_match}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{image\_to\_catalog\_match}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{max\_image\_catalog\_sep}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{Quantity}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Matches the science images to the official SPT catalog.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses the center pixel coordinate of the 3.6 um science image to match against the SZ center of the
clusters in the official SPT catalog. Clusters are kept only if their images match an SZ center within the given
maximum separation. If multiple images match to the same SZ center within our max separation then only the
closest match is kept.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{max\_image\_catalog\_sep} \textendash{} Maximum separation allowed between the image center pixel and the SZ center matched in the official SPT
catalog.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{j\_band\_abs\_mag() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{j\_band\_abs\_mag()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.j_band_abs_mag}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{j\_band\_abs\_mag}}}{}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the J\sphinxhyphen{}band absolute magnitudes for use in the Assef et al. (2011) luminosity function.

\sphinxAtStartPar
To compute the J\sphinxhyphen{}band absolute magnitudes, we will use the observed apparent 3.6 um magnitude and assume a
Polleta  QSO2 SED for all objects to K\sphinxhyphen{}correct to the absolute FLAMINGOS J\sphinxhyphen{}band magnitude.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{object\_mask() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{object\_mask()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.object_mask}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{object\_mask}}}{}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs additional masking on the good pixel maps for requested clusters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
If a cluster has a DS9 regions file present in the directory specified as \sphinxtitleref{region\_file\_dir} in initialization
then we will read in the file, and set pixels within the shapes present in the file to \sphinxtitleref{0}.
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The allowable shapes in the regions file are \sphinxtitleref{circle}, \sphinxtitleref{box}, and \sphinxtitleref{ellipse}. An unexpected shape will raise a
KeyError.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#KeyError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{KeyError}}} \textendash{} An error is raised if the shape in the regions file is not one of the allowable shapes.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{object\_selection() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{object\_selection()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.object_selection}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{object\_selection}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{ch1\_bright\_mag}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{ch1\_faint\_mag}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{ch2\_bright\_mag}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{selection\_band\_faint\_mag}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{selection\_band}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}I2\_MAG\_APER4\textquotesingle{}}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Selects the objects in the clusters as AGN subject to a color cut.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Reads in the SExtractor catalogs and performs all necessary cuts to select the AGN in the cluster.}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
First, a cut is made on the Source Extractor flag requiring an extraction flag of \sphinxtitleref{\textless{} 4}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A magnitude cut is applied on the faint\sphinxhyphen{}end of the selection band. This is determined such that the
completeness limit in the selection band is kept above 80\%.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A magnitude cut is applied to the bright\sphinxhyphen{}end of both bands to remain under the saturation limit.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Finally, the surviving objects’ positions are checked against the good pixel map to ensure that the object
lies on an acceptable location.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Further selection refinement is handled by {\hyperref[\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.selection_membership}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{selection\_membership()}}}}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ch1\_bright\_mag} \textendash{} Bright\sphinxhyphen{}end magnitude threshold for 3.6 um band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ch1\_faint\_mag} \textendash{} Faint\sphinxhyphen{}end magnitude threshold for 3.6 um band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{ch2\_bright\_mag} \textendash{} Bright\sphinxhyphen{}end magnitude threshold for 4.5 um band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{selection\_band\_faint\_mag} \textendash{} Faint\sphinxhyphen{}end magnitude threshold for the specified selection band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{selection\_band} \textendash{} Column name in Source Extractor catalog specifying the selection band to use. Defaults to the 4.5 um, 4”
aperture magnitude photometry.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{object\_separations() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{object\_separations()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

Calculates the separations of each object relative to the SZ center.

Finds both the angular separations and physical separations relative to the cluster’s \(r_{500}\) radius.

\index{selection\_membership() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method)@\spxentry{selection\_membership()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectIRAGN method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectIRAGN.selection_membership}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{selection\_membership}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{color\_threshold}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Iterable}{Iterable}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculates fuzzy degree of membership of each object to be an AGN.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the AGN selection membership for each object in the sample by first incorporating the color error to
remove the Eddington bias—where we are likely to have truly blue objects scatter into our sample. We then
calculate a fuzzy degree of membership for the AGN sample.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{color\_threshold} \textendash{} Manually specify a {[}3.6{]} \sphinxhyphen{} {[}4.5{]} color threshold to be used. If given, the selection membership function
will not use any catalog redshift information to determine the color threshold to be used. Instead, a flat
color threshold will be applied to the entire catalog sample for each value in the color\_threshold iterable.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The selection membership acts as effectively a degree of belief that an object should be included in a sample of
AGN. It is computed as an alpha\sphinxhyphen{}cut on a fuzzy set membership function given by,
\begin{equation*}
\begin{split}\mu_{\mathrm{AGN}} =
    \frac{\int_{\alpha}^{\infty} \mathcal{N}(\mathcal{C}_{12},\delta\mathcal{C}_{12}) d\mathcal{C}_{12}}
    {\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \mathcal{N}(\mathcal{C}_{12}, \delta\mathcal{C}_{12}) d\mathcal{C}_{12}}\end{split}
\end{equation*}
\sphinxAtStartPar
where \(\mathcal{C}_{12}\) and \(\delta\mathcal{C}_{12}\) are the {[}3.6{]} \sphinxhyphen{} {[}4.5{]} color and color
errors respectively of each object, and \(\\alpha\) is either a redshift\sphinxhyphen{}dependent sample purity\sphinxhyphen{}based color
threshold or a flat color threshold depending on the user input.

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the J\sphinxhyphen{}band absolute magnitudes for use in the Assef et al. (2011) luminosity function.

\sphinxAtStartPar
To compute the J\sphinxhyphen{}band absolute magnitudes, we will use the observed apparent 3.6 um magnitude and assume a
Polleta  QSO2 SED for all objects to K\sphinxhyphen{}correct to the absolute FLAMINGOS J\sphinxhyphen{}band magnitude.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{completeness\_value() (SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectFullFieldSDWFS method)@\spxentry{completeness\_value()}\spxextra{SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline\_functions.SelectFullFieldSDWFS method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectFullFieldSDWFS.completeness_value}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{completeness\_value}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{selection\_band}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}I2\_MAG\_APER4\textquotesingle{}}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{mean}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adds completeness simulation data to the catalog.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Takes the completeness curve values for the cluster, interpolates a function between the discrete values, then
queries the specified magnitude of the selected objects in the Source Extractor catalog and adds two columns:
The completeness value of that object at its magnitude and the completeness correction value
\sphinxtitleref{(1/{[}completeness value{]})}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{selection\_band} \textendash{} Column name in Source Extractor catalog specifying the selection band to use. Must be the same band used in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object\_selection()}}. Defaults to the 4.5 um, 4” aperture magnitude photometry.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api/selector:SPTclIRAGN.Pipeline_functions.SelectFullFieldSDWFS.file_pairing}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{file\_pairing}}}{\emph{\DUrole{n}{include}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Iterable}{Iterable}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}, \emph{\DUrole{n}{exclude}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Iterable}{Iterable}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{  }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Self}{Self}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Collates the different files for each cluster into a dictionary.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This will also remove any clusters we request and perform a check that all clusters have the required files.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{include} \textendash{} A list containing the names of the clusters we wish to filter for from the original sample.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{exclude} \textendash{} A list containing the names of the clusters we wish to remove from our sample.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#UserWarning}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{UserWarning}}} \textendash{} A warning is printed if a cluster is missing any necessary files. These clusters will be automatically
    removed from the sample.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The list provided by \sphinxtitleref{include} is filtered for first before the \sphinxtitleref{exclude} list is processed. This allows the
sample to be filtered for a set of clusters we want to run the selection on while still allowing us to mark
certain clusters to be removed from the sample.

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs miscentering operations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Offsets the center within the sigma offset of the positional uncertainty relative to the true cluster center.
This allows for tests of the sensitivity of the radial parameter posteriors with respect to the accuracy of the
position of the cluster center.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{sigma\_offset} \textendash{} Factor of the cluster positional uncertainty to sample a new center within.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{true\_center} \textendash{} The true cluster center as measured by the SZ signal.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{object\_coords} \textendash{} Coordinates of all objects for which we want to calculate radial distances from the new (offset) center.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{theta\_beam} \textendash{} The beam size of the telescope in the detection band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{theta\_core} \textendash{} The SZ beta\sphinxhyphen{}model scale angular radius of the cluster.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{xi} \textendash{} The SZ detection significance of the cluster.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{redshift} \textendash{} The cluster redshift.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{r500} \textendash{} The R500 radius of the cluster.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The offset cluster center and corresponding radial distances in both units of arcminutes and cluster R500.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{tuple}{[}SkyCoord, u.Quantity, u.Quantity{]}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{lower\_dx, lower\_dy} \textendash{} Lower bound on x\sphinxhyphen{} and y\sphinxhyphen{}axes respectively. If not provided, a default of 0 will be used

\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates a catalog of cluster line\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}sights with the specified input parameters.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{miscentering\_offsets}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{num\_clusters} \textendash{} Number of clusters to generate in each catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{mask\_files} \textendash{} List of empirical masks to draw mock clusters from.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{theta\_true} \textendash{} Cluster amplitude parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{eta\_true} \textendash{} Redshift power\sphinxhyphen{}law slope parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{zeta\_true} \textendash{} Mass power\sphinxhyphen{}law slope parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{beta\_true} \textendash{} Radial beta\sphinxhyphen{}model slope parameter value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{rc\_true} \textendash{} Radial beta\sphinxhyphen{}model scale parameter value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{max\_radius} \textendash{} Maximum radius in units of r500 to generate objects along each field of view.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{irac\_45\_bright\_mag} \textendash{} Bright\sphinxhyphen{}end cut in 4.5 um used in empirical AGN selection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{irac\_45\_faint\_mag} \textendash{} Faint\sphinxhyphen{}end cut in 4.5 um used in empirical AGN selection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_delta\_z} \textendash{} Redshift range used to select for objects in \sphinxtitleref{galaxy\_data\_catalog} for cluster objects. Satisfies
\(|z - z_{\rm cl}| \leq \delta z\).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{sz\_theta\_beam} \textendash{} SPT 150 GHz beam size. Used for miscentering analyses.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{miscentering\_offsets} \textendash{} Factors of cluster positional uncertainty on which to perform miscentering analyses.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{output\_dir} \textendash{} Directory to place the output catalog files in.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates a suite of mock catalogs over a range of redshift and mass parameter values. Uses a targeted SNR to
determine the cluster amplitude parameter.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{num\_clusters} \textendash{} Number of clusters to generate in each catalog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{mask\_files} \textendash{} List of empirical masks to draw mock clusters from.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{snr\_target} \textendash{} Specified signal\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}noise ratio that determines the value of the cluster amplitude parameter,
\(\\theta\) given redshift and mass parameters, \(\eta\) and \(\zeta\) respectively.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{snr\_theta} \textendash{} A dictionary containing the fits of SNR\textendash{}theta for various eta and zeta parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{eta\_range} \textendash{} Range of redshift parameter values to generate each catalog in the suite.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{zeta\_range} \textendash{} Range of mass parameter values to generate each catalog in the suite.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{beta\_true} \textendash{} Radial beta\sphinxhyphen{}model slope parameter value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{rc\_true} \textendash{} Radial beta\sphinxhyphen{}model scale parameter value.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{max\_radius} \textendash{} Maximum radius in units of r500 to generate objects along each field of view.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{irac\_45\_bright\_mag} \textendash{} Bright\sphinxhyphen{}end cut in 4.5 um used in empirical AGN selection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{irac\_45\_faint\_mag} \textendash{} Faint\sphinxhyphen{}end cut in 4.5 um used in empirical AGN selection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_delta\_z} \textendash{} Redshift range used to select for objects in \sphinxtitleref{galaxy\_data\_catalog} for cluster objects. Satisfies
\(|z - z_{\rm cl} | \leq \delta z\).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{sz\_theta\_beam} \textendash{} SPT 150 GHz beam size. Used for miscentering analyses.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{miscentering\_offsets} \textendash{} Factors of cluster positional uncertainty on which to perform miscentering analyses.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{output\_dir} \textendash{} Directory to place the output catalog files in.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\chapter{Bayesian Modeling Fitting}
\label{\detokenize{api/fitting:bayesian-modeling-fitting}}\label{\detokenize{api/fitting:fitting}}\label{\detokenize{api/fitting::doc}}

\section{Preprocessing Functions}
\label{\detokenize{api/fitting:module-SPTclIRAGN.SPT_AGN_emcee_preprocessing}}\label{\detokenize{api/fitting:preprocessing-functions}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!SPTclIRAGN.SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing@\spxentry{SPTclIRAGN.SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing}}\index{SPTclIRAGN.SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing@\spxentry{SPTclIRAGN.SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing.py
Author: Benjamin Floyd

\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs the GPF and cluster dictionary construction as a preprocessing step to the MCMC sampling. Results are stored in
a JSON file for later use.
\index{generate\_catalog\_dict() (in module SPTclIRAGN.SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing)@\spxentry{generate\_catalog\_dict()}\spxextra{in module SPTclIRAGN.SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_preprocessing}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parses the input catalog into a dictionary structure containing only the necessary information for the MCMC
sampler.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster} \textendash{} Line of sight catalog to be processed.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{cluster\_id} \textendash{} Name of the cluster, will be used as a key.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{cluster\_dict} \textendash{} Database of all relevant data related to the cluster for the sampler.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the fraction of unmasked pixels within an annulus.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{r} \textendash{} Radial axis on which we will form our annuli.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{z} \textendash{} Redshift of the cluster.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{r500} \textendash{} R500 radius of the cluster.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{center} \textendash{} Center of the cluster.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_id} \textendash{} Cluster name/ID.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{rescale\_factor} \textendash{} Factor by which to rescale the original image. This rebins the pixels of the image into pixels of size
\sphinxtitleref{old\_pix / rescale\_factor}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A list of the fractional area within each annulus that is unmasked.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
good\_pix\_frac

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
SPT\_AGN\_emcee\_sampler\_MPI.py
Author: Benjamin Floyd

\sphinxAtStartPar
This script will preform the Bayesian analysis on the SPT\sphinxhyphen{}AGN data to produce the posterior probability distributions
for all fitting parameters.

Log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood function. It is a modified compound spatial Poisson point process comprising of two parts, the
inhomogeneous cluster term and a homogeneous background term.

\end{description}\end{quote}

Our generating model. It is used as the intensity function of our Poisson likelihood function.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{params} \textendash{} Tuple of (theta, eta, zeta, beta, rc, C)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_id} \textendash{} SPT ID of our cluster in the catalog dictionary

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{r\_r500} \textendash{} A vector of radii of objects within the cluster normalized by the cluster’s r500

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{j\_mag} \textendash{} A vector of J\sphinxhyphen{}band absolute magnitudes to be used in the luminosity function

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{integral} \textendash{} Flag indicating if the luminosity function factor of the model should be integrated. Defaults to \sphinxtitleref{False}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar

model
